cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Transfer of plants, animals, diseases, peoples, and cultures between the Eastern and Western Hemispheres following 1492

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Forced transportation of approximately 12 million Africans to the Americas to provide labor for plantation economies (1500–1866)

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish colonial system granting settlers the right to demand tribute and forced labor from indigenous peoples

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Economic theory that national wealth depends on accumulating gold and silver through favorable trade balances

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Atlantic trade pattern: European goods to Africa, enslaved Africans to the Americas, and colonial raw materials to Europe

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The horrific transatlantic voyage endured by enslaved Africans from West Africa to the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish military adventurers who conquered the Aztec and Inca empires using firearms, horses, steel, and indigenous allies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Massive silver mine in present-day Bolivia that produced enormous wealth for the Spanish Empire

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Agricultural system based on large-scale cash crop production (sugar, tobacco, cotton) using enslaved or coerced labor

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Business organization that pooled investor capital to fund colonial ventures, such as the Dutch East India Company

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish trade route (1565–1815) connecting Manila in the Philippines to Acapulco in Mexico, exchanging Asian goods for American silver

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Small, maneuverable Portuguese sailing ship with lateen sails that enabled exploration along African coasts

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Person of mixed European and indigenous American ancestry in Spanish colonial society

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Hierarchical racial classification system in Spanish colonies ranking people by racial ancestry

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Hernan Cortes
cards.answer: Spanish conquistador who conquered the Aztec Empire (1519–1521) with the help of indigenous allies, firearms, and European diseases

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Francisco Pizarro
cards.answer: Spanish conquistador who conquered the Inca Empire (1532–1533) by capturing Emperor Atahualpa and exploiting Inca civil war

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Bartolome de Las Casas
cards.answer: Spanish friar who documented and condemned the brutal treatment of indigenous peoples in the Americas, advocating for their rights
alternate_answers: Advocate for indigenous rights in the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Vasco da Gama
cards.answer: Portuguese explorer who sailed directly from Europe to India via the Cape of Good Hope (1498), opening the sea route to Asian trade
alternate_answers: First European to sail to India

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Columbus's first voyage to the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Vasco da Gama reaches India by sea

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish conquest of the Aztec Empire by Cortes

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire by Pizarro

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Founding of Jamestown, the first permanent English settlement in America

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Columbian Exchange
cards.answer: The transfer of plants, animals, diseases, peoples, and cultures between the Eastern and Western Hemispheres following Columbus's voyages, fundamentally transforming both worlds

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Mercantilism
cards.answer: Economic theory holding that national power depends on accumulating precious metals through exports exceeding imports, driving colonial extraction policies

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Middle Passage
cards.answer: The transatlantic voyage of enslaved Africans from West Africa to the Americas, marked by extreme overcrowding, disease, and high mortality rates

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Spanish-born colonists at the top of the colonial social hierarchy in the Americas

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: People of European descent born in the Americas who held high social status but below peninsulares

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: The Dutch trading company that monopolized European trade with Asia, functioning as a quasi-governmental authority in Southeast Asia

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Encomienda
cards.answer: Spanish colonial labor system granting settlers the right to demand tribute and forced labor from indigenous peoples in exchange for supposed protection and religious instruction

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Blending of African, indigenous, and European cultural and religious traditions in colonial Latin America

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: 1494 agreement between Spain and Portugal that divided the non-Christian world into two spheres of exploration and colonization

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Christopher Columbus
cards.answer: Genoese explorer whose 1492 voyage to the Americas for Spain initiated sustained European colonization and the Columbian Exchange

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Treaty of Tordesillas dividing colonial claims between Spain and Portugal

cards.subject: ap-world-history
cards.prompt: Person of mixed African and European ancestry in colonial Latin American society
